entries.title: EPISTLE TO THE PHILIPPIANS 2.22-24 on philippians 2:22
entries.excerpt: He says "he served with me," not "he served me."

entries.title: Homily on Philippians 9 on philippians 2:22
entries.excerpt:
"Ye know the proof of him, that as a child serveth a father, so he served with me in furtherance of the Gospel."

And that I speak not at random, "ye yourselves," he says, "know, that as a child serveth a father, so he served with me in furtherance of the Gospel." He presents then Timothy to them, and with reason, that he might enjoy much honor from them. This too he does when he writes to the Corinthians, and he says, "Let no man therefore despise him, for he worketh the work of the Lord as I also do." This he said not as caring for him, but for those who receive him, that they might receive a great reward.

entries.title: Commentary on Philippians on philippians 2:22
entries.excerpt: You yourselves, he says, are witnesses that he served me as a son serves a father, not only in bodily needs, but, what is far more important, in the preaching of the Gospel. Therefore, as my son, he is worthy of honor, and as a servant of God — all the more so. Through this he commends Timothy to them, not so much bestowing honor on him as doing good for them; for if they heed him as one most worthy, then from his word they will, of course, receive benefit and be deemed worthy of the highest reward.

entries.title: Commentary on Philippians on philippians 2:22
entries.excerpt: And if you would know how he has behaved in regard to me, I answer that, Timothy's worth you know, how as a son with a father he has served with me in the gospel, i.e., as though he were a special son: "I sent to you Timothy, my beloved and faithful child in the Lord" (1 Cor. 4:17).
